behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily about LLM output quality (context-groundedness vs generic text). A stubbed model can always return a fixture that passes, hiding real failures in model behavior. Rejected higher level Workflow because the behavior only requires validating one model-dependent judgment (the draft content), not consistency across multiple model-dependent steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run with a real model on representative email threads and assert the draft contains thread-specific references (e.g., key entities/requests from the input) and fails a genericity rubric when those references are absent.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: Detecting contradiction between natural-language draft claims and KB entries depends on model/ML judgment quality; deterministic mocks can hide this failure mode. Rejected Workflow because this behavior is scoped to one model-dependent check stage, not cross-step agentic consistency.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real model-backed consistency checker with drafts containing known contradictory claims against KB facts, and verify contradictions are flagged while non-contradictions are not.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model behavior under missing-context conditions (choosing uncertainty/disclaimer language vs hallucinating). A mock would mask fabrication risk. Rejected Workflow because the requirement is about the draft behavior in this condition, not validating multi-step agentic state consistency.
behaviors.test_description: With KB retrieval returning no relevant facts, run real-model draft generation and assert presence of required disclaimer language and absence of unsupported factual assertions.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is about an externally visible side effect at the email boundary (send/no-send). Per round-trip side-effect rule, unit/integration-only checks (e.g., mocked sender calls) can give false confidence; you need real boundary verification that no message is sent before approval. Rejected Agentic because no model-output-quality judgment is required by this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the flow against a real send boundary (sandbox mailbox/API), verify no outbound email appears before explicit approval, then verify send occurs only after approval via outbox/readback observation.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly requires a multi-step pipeline with model-dependent stages in sequence (generate + factual check) and end-to-end state handoff, which exceeds single-step Agentic testing. Rejected lower levels because they cannot validate cross-step consistency of outputs and decisions through the full pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run the complete pipeline on a real email thread: ingest thread, generate draft with real model, run real consistency check, and confirm the reviewed draft presented for approval reflects prior step outputs correctly across the sequence.
